buggy_code:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv('returns.csv', parse_dates=['date'], index_col='date')

# Reset index converts DatetimeIndex to integer
df_reset = df.reset_index()

# Create boolean mask on original df
mask = df['returns'] > 0

# Apply mask to reset DataFrame — index mismatch!
positive_returns = df_reset[mask]

print(positive_returns.head())
clean_code:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv('returns.csv', parse_dates=['date'], index_col='date')

# Filter BEFORE reset_index to preserve alignment
positive_returns = df[df['returns'] > 0].reset_index()

print(positive_returns.head())
description: Boolean mask from original DataFrame applied to reset_index version with different index.
explanation: df has a DatetimeIndex. After reset_index(), df_reset has a RangeIndex (0, 1, 2...). The boolean mask from df uses DatetimeIndex positions. Applying this mask to df_reset aligns on integer positions, not dates. If any rows were dropped or reordered, this silently selects wrong rows.
suggested_fix: Always filter before resetting the index, or use .loc with the same index on both objects.

issues.title: Boolean mask applied across different index types after reset_index
